name: Parker Washington
headline: Breakout slot man earns flex floor in Jacksonville
body:
Washington took a real third-year leap, turning 95 targets into 58 catches for 847 yards and five scores across 16 games while posting 126.7 fantasy points and a WR24 rank. That nearly doubled his 2024 line of 51 targets, 390 yards and a WR87 finish, and the underlying usage backed it up: target share jumped from 9.8 to 17.4 percent, WOPR from 0.238 to 0.431, and yards per route run from 1.07 to 2.24. He also stretched the field, ranking high in air yards per target (12.49) while posting a 0.333 EPA per play.

The 2023 sixth-round Penn State pick is now Jacksonville's clearest perimeter option, though seven drops and a 61.1 percent catch rate flag inconsistent hands. With a 17.4 percent target share already established and 14 red-zone looks, he carries a startable floor but a ceiling capped by the offense's pass-volume swings. Draft him as a low-end flex with WR3 upside, not a locked weekly starter.
